data.content:
Now I have all the info. Let me create `Directory.Packages.props` and strip versions from all project files.

**Version conflicts resolved (highest wins):**
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 (vs 8.0.4)
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 (vs 1.11.4)

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me collect all package references and resolve conflicts using the highest version:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests | Highest |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - | 1.13.2 |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - | 8.0.1 |


| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - | 1.15.0 |
| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 | 2.9.3 |

Now I need to create a centralized package management file with all these highest versions enabled, then strip out the individual version declarations from each project file.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Created `Directory.Packages.props` with Central Package Management enabled. Two version conflicts were resolved to the highest version:

- **System.Text.Json**: `10.0.1` (Worker had `8.0.4`)
- **Azure.Identity**: `1.13.2` (Shared had `1.11.4`)

All `Version` attributes removed from the 4 `.csproj` files; versions are now centrally managed.
